title: fraxinus nigra var. sambucifolia
intro: Fraxinus nigra var. sambucifolia, also known as the black ash, is a deciduous tree species native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Fraxinus nigra var. sambucifolia include reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and alleviating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
